$schema_notes: Seed data for an inspection helper app. Two top-level domains: home_inspection and boat_survey. Each inspectable area has items grouped by action type. Item types: 'inspect' = surveyor must examine/operate it; 'describe' = record its type/characteristics in the report; 'report_if' = defect conditions that must be flagged as needing correction; 'not_required' = explicit exclusions (useful for scoping disclaimers / 'out of scope' UI). Every item has a stable id (area_code + type letter + index) so it can map cleanly to DB rows / checkboxes.

meta.source: InterNACHI International Standards of Practice for Performing a General Home Inspection
meta.license_note: InterNACHI publishes this SoP under a free-use license and offers a 'create your own clean copy' option. Item text below is rephrased into terse checklist form rather than copied verbatim.
meta.definition: A non-invasive, visual examination of the accessible areas of a residential property, performed for a fee, to identify material defects that are both observed and deemed material by the inspector.
meta.scope_note: Applies to properties with four or fewer residential units and their attached garages/carports. Visual only. Not technically exhaustive. Not a code-compliance inspection.
meta.alternate_standard: ASHI (American Society of Home Inspectors) publishes a parallel Standard of Practice covering the same core systems; it is more prescriptive in places, does not mandate photos, and does not require AFCI/GFCI testing. Many state licensing boards reference one or both.

global_limitations: Will not identify concealed or latent defects.
Does not cover aesthetic / cosmetic / matters-of-taste concerns.
Does not determine suitability for any use.
Does not determine market value or marketability.
Does not advise for or against purchase.
Does not determine life expectancy of property or systems.
Does not include items not permanently installed.
Based on observations on the inspection date only; not a prediction of future conditions.

not_required_to_determine: Condition of anything not readily accessible.
Service life expectancy of any component or system.
Size, capacity, BTU, performance or efficiency of any system.
Cause or reason of any condition.
Cause of the need for any correction/repair/replacement.
Presence of pests (rodents, birds, bats, insects, etc.).
Presence of mold, mildew or fungus.
Presence of airborne hazards including radon.
Environmental hazards (lead paint, asbestos, toxic drywall).
not_required_to_operate: Any system that is shut down.
Any system that does not function properly.
Low-voltage systems (phone, cable, satellite, antennae, lights, remotes).
Any system that does not turn on with normal operating controls.
Moisture meters, gas detectors or similar equipment.

areas.name: Roof

inspect.text: Roof-covering materials (from ground level or eaves)
Gutters
Downspouts
Vents, flashing, skylights, chimney and other roof penetrations
General roof structure from readily accessible panels/doors/stairs

report_if.text: Observed indications of active roof leaks

not_required: Inspect underground downspout diverter drainage pipes.
Inspect antennae, satellite dishes, lightning arresters, de-icing equipment.

areas.name: Exterior

inspect.text: Exterior wall-covering materials
Eaves, soffits and fascia
Representative number of windows
All exterior doors
Flashing and trim
Adjacent walkways and driveways
Stairs, steps, stoops, stairways and ramps
Porches, patios, decks, balconies and carports
Railings, guards and handrails
Vegetation, surface drainage, retaining walls and grading where they may affect the structure via moisture intrusion

report_if.text: Improper spacing between intermediate balusters, spindles and rails

not_required: Inspect/operate screens, storm windows, shutters, awnings, fences, outbuildings, accent lighting.
Inspect items not visible/accessible from the ground (incl. window/door flashing).
Inspect solar, wind or geothermal systems.
Determine integrity of multi-pane glazing or thermal seals.

areas.name: Basement, Foundation, Crawl Space & Structure

inspect.text: Foundation
Basement
Crawl space
Structural components

describe.text: Location of access to the under-floor crawl space

report_if.text: Wood in contact with or near soil
Active water penetration
Possible foundation movement (sheetrock/brick cracks, out-of-square door frames, unlevel floors)
Cutting/notching/boring of framing members posing a structural or safety concern

not_required: Enter crawl spaces not readily accessible or where entry risks damage/hazard.
Operate sump pumps with inaccessible floats.
Determine size/spacing/span/adequacy of foundation bolting, bracing, joists or supports.
Report on adequacy of any structural system or component.

areas.name: Heating

inspect.text: Heating system, using normal operating controls

report_if.text: Heating system that did not operate
Heating system deemed inaccessible

not_required: Inspect/measure interiors of flues, chimneys, fire chambers, heat exchangers, combustion-air systems, humidifiers, dehumidifiers, electronic air filters, geothermal or solar heating.
Inspect fuel tanks or concealed fuel supply systems.
Activate heating when ambient conditions risk unsafe operation or equipment damage.

areas.name: Cooling

inspect.text: Cooling system, using normal operating controls

report_if.text: Cooling system that did not operate
Cooling system deemed inaccessible

not_required: Inspect portable window/through-wall units or electronic air filters.
Operate equipment when exterior temp is below 65°F or conditions risk damage/unsafe operation.
Examine electrical current, coolant fluids/gases, or coolant leakage.

areas.name: Plumbing

inspect.text: Main water supply shut-off valve
Main fuel supply shut-off valve
Water heating equipment (energy source, venting, TPR/Watts 210 valves, seismic bracing)
Interior water supply incl. all fixtures and faucets, by running water
All toilets, by flushing
All sinks, tubs and showers for functional drainage
Drain, waste and vent system
Drainage sump pumps with accessible floats

describe.text: Whether water supply is public or private (per observed evidence)
Location of main water supply shut-off valve
Location of main fuel supply shut-off valve
Location of any observed fuel-storage system
Capacity of water heating equipment, if labeled

report_if.text: Water supply deficiencies (functional flow with two fixtures run simultaneously)
Hot/cold faucet installation deficiencies
Active plumbing water leaks observed
Toilets damaged, loose at floor, leaking, or with non-operating tank components

not_required: Inspect flue/chimney interiors, combustion air, water softeners/filters, well pumps/tanks, floor drains, lawn or fire sprinklers.
Inspect clothes washers or their connections.
Test shower pans / tub-shower surrounds for leakage.
Determine effectiveness of anti-siphon / backflow / drain-stop devices.
Inspect wastewater / water-treatment systems or filters.
Inspect water storage tanks, pressure or bladder tanks.
Determine presence/condition of polybutylene or similar plastic piping.
Inspect or test for gas/fuel leaks.

areas.name: Electrical

inspect.text: Service drop
Overhead service conductors and attachment point
Service head, gooseneck and drip loops
Service mast, conduit and raceway
Electric meter and base
Service-entrance conductors
Main service disconnect
Panelboards and over-current devices (breakers and fuses)
Service grounding and bonding
Representative number of switches, fixtures and receptacles, incl. AFCI test where possible
GFCI receptacles and breakers, tested where possible
Presence of smoke and carbon monoxide detectors

describe.text: Main service disconnect amperage rating, if labeled

report_if.text: Service-entrance conductor insulation / drip loop / clearance deficiencies
Unfilled circuit-breaker panel openings
Solid-conductor aluminum branch wiring, if readily visible
Receptacle faults: no power, reversed polarity, missing cover, faulty GFCI, arcing/heat, ungrounded, or loose
Absence of smoke and/or carbon monoxide detectors

not_required: Insert tools/probes into panelboards or fixtures.
Operate systems that are shut down.
Operate or reset over-current / overload devices.
Operate or test smoke/CO detectors or alarms.
Inspect/test security, fire or signaling systems.
Measure amperage/voltage of service equipment if unlabeled.
Inspect ancillary wiring or remote-control devices.
Inspect low-voltage systems, de-icing tape, pool wiring or time-controlled devices.
Inspect generators, windmills, PV solar, or battery storage.

areas.name: Fireplace

inspect.text: Readily accessible/visible portions of fireplaces and chimneys
Lintels above fireplace openings
Damper doors, by opening/closing if accessible and operable
Cleanout doors and frames

report_if.text: Joint separation/damage/deterioration of hearth, hearth extension or chambers
Manually operated dampers that did not open/close
Lack of a smoke detector in the same room
Lack of a CO detector in the same room
Cleanouts not made of non-combustible material

not_required: Inspect flue/vent system or chimney/flue interiors, fire doors, seals, mantels.
Determine need for a chimney sweep.
Inspect automatic fuel-fed or combustion/make-up air devices.
Move inserts, stoves or firebox contents.
Perform smoke test or NFPA-style / Phase I inspection.

areas.name: Attic, Insulation & Ventilation

inspect.text: Insulation in unfinished spaces (attics, crawl spaces, foundation areas)
Ventilation of unfinished spaces
Mechanical exhaust systems in kitchen, bathrooms and laundry

describe.text: Approximate average insulation depth at unfinished attic floor / roof structure

report_if.text: General absence of insulation or ventilation in unfinished spaces

not_required: Enter attic/unfinished spaces not readily accessible or where entry risks damage/hazard.
Damage finish/weather seal around access panels.
Identify composition or R-value of insulation.

areas.name: Doors, Windows & Interior

inspect.text: Representative number of doors and windows, by opening/closing
Floors, walls and ceilings
Stairs, steps, landings, stairways and ramps
Railings, guards and handrails
Garage vehicle doors and openers, using normal operating controls

describe.text: Garage vehicle door as manual or opener-equipped

report_if.text: Improper baluster/spindle/rail spacing on steps, stairways, guards, railings
Photo-electric garage safety sensors that did not operate properly
Any window obviously fogged or showing broken-seal evidence

not_required: Move furniture/stored items/coverings to inspect concealed floor structure.
Inspect elevators, pools, spas or fountains.

meta.structure_note: Unlike home inspection, marine surveying has NO single public line-by-line Standard of Practice and NO licensing. Surveyors instead (a) hold an accreditation, (b) follow their body's voluntary report-content guidelines, and (c) evaluate the vessel against published technical standards (ABYC, NFPA, USCG/CFR). The 'system_checklist' below is a practical inspection checklist synthesized from typical pre-purchase survey scope and ABYC system domains — it is NOT a verbatim published standard, but is structured to mirror how the home_inspection data works so both can share UI.

accrediting_bodies.name: National Association of Marine Surveyors
accrediting_bodies.report_guideline: Recommended Guidelines for Yacht Condition and Valuation Surveys
accrediting_bodies.note: Min. ~5 yrs full-time experience + exam. Specializations: Yacht & Small Craft, Hull & Machinery, Cargo.

accrediting_bodies.name: Society of Accredited Marine Surveyors
accrediting_bodies.report_guideline: Recommended Survey Report Content / Survey Practice Recommendations
accrediting_bodies.note: Min. ~5 yrs experience + exam + continuing education. Surveyor Associate (SA) is the pre-accreditation tier.

reference_standards.name: American Boat & Yacht Council
reference_standards.covers: The authoritative reference. ~68 standards / 650+ pages covering deck hardware, hull, electrical (AC/DC), fuel, propulsion, steering, etc. Members get 24/7 access.

reference_standards.name: National Fire Protection Association
reference_standards.covers: Fuel and electrical fire-safety standards for boats (esp. NFPA 302).

reference_standards.name: US Coast Guard requirements, 33 & 46 CFR
reference_standards.covers: Federal safety and regulatory compliance (required safety equipment, construction, labeling).

reference_standards.name: American Bureau of Shipping / Lloyd's Register
reference_standards.covers: Hull construction standards, referenced mainly for larger/commercial vessels.

survey_types.name: Pre-Purchase (Condition) Survey
survey_types.purpose: Most comprehensive. Full examination of condition + valuation, for a prospective buyer. Often paired with a sea trial and (for larger vessels) a separate engine/machinery survey.

survey_types.name: Insurance / Condition & Value (C&V) Survey
survey_types.purpose: Required by underwriters; focuses on safety, seaworthiness and fair market value. Performed to the same standard as a pre-purchase survey.

survey_types.name: Appraisal Survey
survey_types.purpose: Valuation for financing, estate/divorce settlement, or charitable donation. Recognized by IRS, courts, banks and insurers.

survey_types.name: Damage Survey
survey_types.purpose: Determines nature, cause and extent of damage after an incident; performed for insurers, owners or other vested parties.

common_methods: Sound the hull with a phenolic/plastic hammer to detect voids and delamination.
Reference ABYC standards throughout (and cite them in the report).
Test electronics and electrical systems for operability.
Use a moisture meter on the hull/deck (common but not universal).
Conduct a sea trial where possible (assesses propulsion, steering, handling under load).
Haul-out / out-of-water inspection of bottom, running gear, through-hulls and shafts.

inspect.text: Hull exterior topsides and bottom (sounding for voids/delamination)
Moisture readings on hull and deck (cored areas especially)
Keel, keel-to-hull joint, and ballast attachment
Stringers, bulkheads and internal structural members (accessible)
Blisters, cracks, prior repairs, osmosis (FRP) / corrosion (metal) / rot (wood)
Deck, cabin top and hull-to-deck joint
Transom and swim platform

report_if.text: Structural delamination, significant moisture intrusion, or compromised hull-to-deck joint

inspect.text: Cleats, chocks, stanchions, pulpits and rails (backing plates where accessible)
Hatches, ports, windows and seals
Lifelines, rails and guards
Non-skid and deck surface condition
All through-hull fittings and seacocks (operation and bonding)
Hose clamps, hoses and double-clamping below waterline
Shaft, strut, cutless bearing and shaft seal
Propeller(s) and rudder(s)
Sacrificial anodes (zincs) condition

report_if.text: Seized/corroded seacocks, single-clamped below-waterline hoses, or excessive shaft/bearing play

system_checklist.name: Propulsion & Machinery

inspect.text: Engine(s) external condition, mounts and alignment
Belts, hoses, exhaust system and risers
Transmission/gearcase and controls
Cooling system (raw-water and closed-loop)
Operation under load during sea trial (smoke, temp, RPM, vibration)

not_required: Internal engine teardown or oil analysis (recommend a separate engine/machinery surveyor for high-hour or large vessels).

inspect.text: Tanks, mounting and labeling
Fuel lines, fittings, filters and shut-offs
Fill, vent and anti-siphon arrangements
Conformance with ABYC / NFPA fuel-system standards

report_if.text: Fuel leaks, non-compliant lines/fittings, or improper tank installation

inspect.text: DC system: batteries, switches, fusing/breakers, wiring condition
AC system: shore power inlet, panel, GFCI/ELCI protection, polarity
Wiring runs, support, chafe protection and connections
Bonding and galvanic/cathodic protection system
Conformance with ABYC electrical standards (E-11 etc.)

report_if.text: Improper/undersized wiring, missing overcurrent protection, AC reverse polarity, or unsafe shore-power connection

inspect.text: Fresh water system, tanks, pumps and fixtures
Bilge pumps (manual and automatic) and high-water alarms
Marine sanitation device (MSD), holding tank and overboard valves
Hoses, clamps and anti-siphon loops
Steering system (hydraulic/mechanical/cable) and play
Rudder bearings, stuffing box and linkages
Engine/throttle/shift controls
Autopilot, if fitted
Anchor(s), rode, chain and connections
Windlass operation
Dock lines and fenders
PFDs (count, type, condition)
Fire extinguishers / fixed suppression (charge, mounting, certification)
Flares and visual distress signals (in date)
Navigation lights and sound-signaling device
Required placards (oil, garbage, MSD), registration and HIN
CO detectors and propane/LPG system safety (if fitted)
EPIRB / liferaft, where applicable

report_if.text: Missing/expired required safety equipment or non-compliance with USCG carriage requirements

inspect.text: GPS/chartplotter, radar, depth sounder, VHF (operability)
Compass and instrumentation
Antennas and mounting

boat_survey.valuation_note: Pre-purchase, insurance and appraisal surveys conclude with a value opinion (fair market value and/or replacement cost). For app purposes this is a separate report section from the condition findings; surveyors typically recommend sharing only the Findings & Recommendations with the seller, not the valuation.
